mechanism_narrative: A sustained elevated level of inflation, as measured by CPIAUCSL above 295, forces the Federal Reserve to maintain a hawkish stance, compressing real yields and pushing nominal 10-year Treasury yields higher over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Inflation at multidecade high in September 2022
failure_condition: Over any rolling 12-month window, the signal generates positive 20-day forward yield returns in fewer than 50% of observations when CPIAUCSL > 295, or the Rank IC of CPIAUCSL with respect to 20-day yield changes is below 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate (UNRATE) is at or below 3.5%, indicating an extremely tight labor market, wage pressures and aggregate demand support higher long-term interest rates as the market prices in sustained economic strength and potential overheating.
evidence_refs: fact:Unemployment at 3.5% in September 2022, near historic lows
failure_condition: If in the 6 months following a trigger event, the average 20-day yield change is negative or the signal produces positive returns in less than 60% of cases, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: The joint effect of high inflation (CPIAUCSL) and a rising federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) amplifies the upward pressure on 10-year yields, as the market adjusts to a persistently restrictive monetary stance that raises term premiums.
evidence_refs: fact:Both inflation and Fed funds were elevated in Q3 2022
failure_condition: The Rank IC of the interaction term (CPIAUCSL * FEDFUNDS) with respect to 20-day yield changes is below 0.06 over any 2-year rolling window, or the partial correlation of each proxy individually is not both positive.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-pandemic recovery regime with strong consumer demand, rising retail sales (RSAFS) signal economic momentum that pushes 10-year yields higher as growth expectations reprice. In a recessionary regime, the opposite relationship holds.
evidence_refs: fact:Retail sales were robust at $683.974B in August 2022
regime_hint: expansionary phase with strong consumer demand
failure_condition: If in any rolling 12-month window where NBER recession indicator is absent, the monthly change in RSAFS has a negative correlation with 20-day yield changes, or the signal produces yield declines in more than 50% of months, reject.

mechanism_narrative: A very high level of nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS above 150,000) indicates labor demand exceeding supply, which leads to wage inflation and higher long-term bond yields as the market fears cost-push inflation and a tight policy response.
evidence_refs: fact:Payrolls at 153,018 in September 2022, well above 150K threshold
failure_condition: Over the next 12 months, if the average 20-day yield return following PAYEMS releases >150K is not positive and significantly different from zero at the 5% level, reject the hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) above 1.5 million units reflect strong construction demand, which feeds into GDP growth and inflationary pressures, causing the 10-year yield to rise as real growth expectations increase.
evidence_refs: fact:Housing starts were 1,575 in August 2022, above 1.5M
failure_condition: If in the 6 months following each trigger event, the forward 20-day yield change is negative in more than 50% of occurrences, or the average change is not positive, reject.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in industrial production (INDPRO) reflects a heated supply side that drives up capacity utilization and commodity prices, leading the market to anticipate tighter monetary policy and higher long-term yields over a quarterly horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:Industrial production index at 104.55 in August 2022, above 104
failure_condition: If the correlation between INDPRO monthly growth and 60-day yield changes is negative over any 3-year rolling window, or the signal fails to produce positive yield returns in more than 60% of triggered quarters, reject.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of a high federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS > 2.5%) and low unemployment (UNRATE < 3.6%) signals an overheating economy that compels the Fed to continue hiking, pushing 10-year yields up over a 20-day horizon as the market prices in further tightening.
evidence_refs: fact:Fed funds at 2.56% and unemployment at 3.5% in September 2022
failure_condition: If in any 12-month period with the trigger active, the average 20-day yield change is negative or the signal produces positive returns less than 60% of the time, reject the hypothesis.
